bot_not_found: 🔎 Bot not found, or you do not have access.
language.title: 🌐 Language Choose the language BotMother should use for menus and messages.
language.changed: ✅ Language updated BotMother will use English for your menus and messages.
language.changed_my: ✅ ဘာသာစကား ပြောင်းပြီးပါပြီ BotMother သည် သင့် menu နှင့် message များအတွက် မြန်မာဘာသာကို အသုံးပြုပါမည်။
help.text: 🤖 BotMother Build, run, inspect, and edit Telegram bots from plain-language prompts. Choose an action below. BotMother will guide only when it needs more detail.
home.title: 🏠 Home Choose what you want to do:
help.create: 🪄 Create Tap New Bot, describe what the child bot should do, answer any necessary AI questions, then paste the child token from @BotFather. Good prompt details • Admin IDs • Payment or contact info • Required buttons and screens • API keys or settings • What users should see first
help.manage: 📦 Manage Tap My Bots, then choose a bot. Each bot page includes status, logs, ask, edit, revise, restart, stop, and delete actions. You do not need to type internal IDs for normal use.
help.ops: 🧰 Operations Use each bot page for dashboard, validation, logs, auto-fix, restart, stop, and delete. Auto Fix reads source, validation results, and recent logs, then asks the AI for a targeted repair. Delete stops the child bot and frees its token for reuse.
help.utils: 🪪 Utilities Profile shows your full Telegram user and chat info. Health shows the manager and child-process summary. Examples gives ready-to-edit prompt ideas.
help.credits: 💳 Credits BotMother uses credits for AI-heavy work and running child bots. Open Credits to see your live balance and the current costs. Status, logs, restart, stop, and delete are free. Admins add credits from the management bot.
help.fallback: ⌨️ Command Fallbacks Buttons are the main interface, but Telegram commands still work when needed: /newbot, /bots, /search, /credits, /status, /logs, /validate, /fix, /ask, /edit, /revise, /restart, /stop, /delete, /id, /health, /cancel.
credits.disabled: 💳 Credits Credits are disabled on this BotMother instance.
credits.exempt: 💳 Credits You are credit-exempt. Owner accounts can use paid AI actions and runtime without consuming credits.
credits.overview: 💳 Credits Balance {balance} credits What uses credits • New Bot: {new_bot} • Edit / Revise / Auto Fix: {edit} • Ask Bot: {ask} • Runtime: 1 per {runtime} accumulated bot-hours Status, logs, restart, stop, and delete are free. Need more credits? Ask an admin to add them from the management bot.
credits.insufficient: 💳 Not enough credits Action {action} Required {cost} credits Your balance {balance} credits Ask an admin to add credits, then try again.
examples.text: ✨ Bot Ideas Copy one, tweak it, then tap New Bot. 🛒 Shop Bot Online store bot with product catalog, cart, KPay payment instructions, order tracking, and admin notifications. Admin IDs are 123456789 and 987654321. 📅 Booking Bot Appointment booking bot for a small clinic. Users choose date/time, leave phone number, and admins can view bookings. 🎓 Quiz Bot Daily quiz bot with scores, leaderboard, hints, and admin command to add questions. 📣 Channel Assistant Bot that drafts announcements, stores reusable templates, and lets admins broadcast to subscribers. Tip: include required admin IDs, payment info, API keys/settings, and any must-have buttons up front.
empty.bots: 🪄 No child bots yet Tap New Bot to create one, or open Examples for ideas.
bots.title: 📦 Your Bots Tap a bot to open actions.
logs.empty: 🧾 No logs yet. Start or restart the bot, then open Logs again.
search.usage: 🔎 Search Type what you want to find after the command. Examples /search shop /search running /search @mybot
search.empty: 🔎 No matching bots Try a bot name, username, owner name, or status such as running.
choose.logs_no_id: 🧾 Choose Logs Pick a bot first.
choose.ask_no_id: 💬 Choose Ask Bot Pick a bot first.
flow.expired: ⌛ This flow expired Please choose the bot again.
cancel.done: ✅ Cancelled Choose a next action below.
newbot.start: 🪄 New Bot Describe the Telegram bot you want to build.
newbot.choose_template: 🪄 New Bot Start with the closest mode. BotMother will use it as a shortcut, not a cage. Flow 1. Choose a mode 2. Describe the bot 3. Answer the AI's necessary questions 4. Paste the child token from @BotFather
newbot.template_shop: 🛒 Shop Bot Brief Send one message with the details below. Include if relevant • What you sell • Admin Telegram user IDs • Payment method, KPay/phone/QR details • Delivery or pickup rules • What happens after an order Example Online store for shoes. Users browse products, add to cart, send phone/address, see KPay instructions, then admins 123456789 and 987654321 get the order.
newbot.template_booking: 📅 Booking Bot Brief Send one message with the details below. Include if relevant • Service name and available times • Required customer info • Admin Telegram user IDs • Approval or automatic confirmation • Cancellation rules Example Clinic booking bot. Users choose service, date, time, name, and phone. Admin 123456789 confirms or rejects bookings.
newbot.template_support: 🎧 Support Bot Brief Send one message with the details below. Include if relevant • Support categories • Admin Telegram user IDs • FAQ answers • Ticket status flow • How admins should reply Example Support bot for an internet service. Users choose Billing, Technical, or Account, describe the issue, and admins 123456789 get tickets.
newbot.template_quiz: 🎓 Quiz Bot Brief Send one message with the details below. Include if relevant • Quiz topic • Question style • Scoring rules • Leaderboard needs • Admin Telegram user IDs Example Daily English vocabulary quiz. Users answer 10 multiple-choice questions, get scores, and see a weekly leaderboard. Admin 123456789 can add questions.
newbot.template_channel: 📣 Channel Assistant Brief Send one message with the details below. Include if relevant • Channel workflow • Draft/template needs • Admin Telegram user IDs • Preview and confirmation rules • Subscriber controls Example Channel assistant for daily promotions. Admins draft posts, preview them, save templates, and confirm before broadcasting.
newbot.template_other: ➕ Custom Bot Brief Send one message describing the bot. Useful details • Who will use it • What buttons/screens it needs • What data it should save • Admin Telegram user IDs • Payment, API keys, or special settings Example A reminder bot for students. Users save homework with due dates, get daily reminders, and can mark tasks done.
newbot.examples_in_flow: ✨ Prompt Examples Short but good A KPay shop bot for phone accessories. Users browse products, add to cart, send name/phone/address, and receive payment instructions. Admin 123456789 gets every order. Operational A booking bot for a barber shop. Users choose service, date, time, and phone number. Admin 123456789 approves bookings and users receive confirmation. Custom A private club bot. Members see events, RSVP, and receive reminders. Admins can add events and export RSVP lists.
newbot.reprompt: ✍️ Start Over Send a fresh description for the child bot. Include any details the AI missed or misunderstood.
newbot.empty_prompt: ✍️ Send the bot brief One clear message is enough. Use the checklist above, or tap Examples.
newbot.thinking: 🧠 Designing the bot... Checking requirements, essential launch data, and a deployable implementation plan.
newbot.more_detail: ⚠️ More detail needed Try New Bot again and include the essentials up front: • Admin IDs • Payment or contact details • API keys or settings • Must-have buttons or commands
newbot.missing_launch_detail: ⚠️ Missing launch detail Try New Bot again with the required data included up front.
newbot.token: ✅ Plan Ready Final step Paste the child bot token from @BotFather. Create a separate bot in @BotFather for this child bot, then paste only that token here. Important Do not paste the mother bot token.
newbot.token_invalid: 🔐 That does not look like a Telegram bot token Paste the full child bot token from @BotFather. Format 123456789:AA... Your plan is still saved. Paste the corrected token.
newbot.token_mother: 🔐 That is the mother bot token Create a separate child bot in @BotFather and paste that child token here. Your plan is still saved.
newbot.token_duplicate: 🔁 Token already attached This token belongs to {title}. Your plan is still saved. Paste a different child token, or cancel and manage the existing bot.
newbot.token_duplicate_other: 🔁 Token already attached That token is already attached to another active bot. Your plan is still saved. Paste a different child token.
newbot.expired: ⌛ Plan expired Tap New Bot to start again.
newbot.launching: 🚀 Launching... Generating, validating, sandboxing, and starting the child bot.
ask.start_examples: 💬 Ask {title} Examples • Why did it stop? • What commands does it support? • How should I edit it to add payments?
ask.start_short: 💬 Ask {title} What do you want to know?
ask.empty: ✍️ Ask a question Tap Cancel to abort.
ask.reading: 🔍 Reading bot context... Checking latest source, status, and recent logs.
choose.none: 🪄 No bots yet Tap New Bot to create one first.
choose.status: 📊 Choose a bot Pick the bot you want to inspect.
choose.ask: 💬 Choose a bot Pick the bot you want to ask about.
choose.edit: ✏️ Choose a bot Pick the bot you want to edit.
choose.revise: ♻️ Choose a bot Pick the bot you want to rebuild from a new prompt.
choose.tail: 🧾 Choose a bot Pick the bot whose logs you want to inspect.
choose.validation: 🧪 Choose a bot Pick the bot whose validation report you want to view.
choose.restart: 🔄 Choose a bot Pick the bot you want to restart.
choose.stop: 🛑 Choose a bot Pick the bot you want to stop.
choose.autofix: 🛠️ Choose a bot Pick the bot you want BotMother to repair from logs.
choose.delete: 🗑️ Choose a bot Pick the bot you want to delete.
source.owner_only: 🔒 Owner Only Raw source is owner-only. Use Edit Bot to change bots with a prompt.
revise.start: ♻️ Revise {title} Send the new full prompt. This regenerates the bot from scratch. For smaller changes, tap Edit instead.
revise.start_short: ♻️ Revise {title} Describe the new complete version. Use this when the bot should be rebuilt from a fresh prompt. For a smaller change, tap Edit.
revise.empty: ✍️ Send a revision prompt Tap Cancel to abort.
revise.running: ♻️ Regenerating... Generating, validating, and restarting the child bot.
edit.start: ✏️ Edit {title} Describe what you want to change. Examples • Add a help menu with examples • Make checkout ask for phone number and address • Improve error messages and admin notifications The AI may ask follow-up questions. Tap Cancel to abort.
edit.start_short: ✏️ Edit {title} Describe what you want to change.
edit.reprompt: ✍️ Re-prompt {title} Send a fresh edit request. Describe the exact change you want, and include any details the AI missed.
edit.empty: ✍️ Describe the change Tap Cancel to abort.
edit.more_detail: ⚠️ More detail needed Try Edit Bot again with the missing details included up front.
edit.applying: 🚀 Applying edit... Generating, validating, and restarting the child bot.
followup.empty: ✍️ Reply with the missing details Tap Cancel to abort.
autofix.running: 🛠️ Auto Fix running... Reading source, validation results, and recent logs, then asking the AI for a targeted repair.
ai.progress: {title} {detail} Elapsed {elapsed}s{tokens} Telegram shows a live draft while the AI works.
ai.progress_tokens_waiting: Token budget {budget} completion tokens Tokens used Waiting for OpenRouter to report usage...
ai.progress_newbot_detail: Understanding the request and deciding whether more details are needed.
ai.progress_readiness_detail: Making sure the plan has the essential data needed before asking for the token.
ai.progress_codegen_detail: The coding model is writing the standalone Python file. Code is not streamed into chat.
ai.progress_ask_detail: Reviewing this bot’s prompt, source, status, and recent logs.
ai.progress_autofix_detail: Reading logs and source, then generating a targeted repair.
ai.progress_revise_detail: Creating a fresh implementation and preparing to restart the child bot.
ai.progress_edit_detail: Checking whether the requested change is clear enough to apply.
ai.progress_edit_apply_detail: The coding model is updating the Python file. Code is not streamed into chat.
health.title: 🩺 BotMother Health
error.unhandled: ⚠️ BotMother hit an unexpected error Error ID {error_id} Try the action again, or open Help for usage examples. If this involved a child bot, the Logs button may show more context.
delete.confirm: 🗑️ Delete {title}? This stops it and frees its token.
